skill.name: terraform-infrastructure
skill.description: Guides Terraform infrastructure workflows for setup, provisioning, modules, state management, environments, CI/CD, and security review.
skill.summary: Plan and review Terraform infrastructure workflows from setup through secure delivery.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown formatting and ordinary Terraform workflow wording. The skill contains only instructional text and copy-paste prompts, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command findings are Markdown inline code or fenced prompt examples that reference other skills. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and these locations contain skill names or prompt text inside backticks. There is no executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation syntax.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Normal Words
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography findings align with ordinary wording such as the description and design steps. No cryptographic algorithm use or implementation appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain Terraform workflow prose, not MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic calls. No code exists in the skill that could perform weak cryptography.

content.value_statement: Terraform projects need clear steps for modules, state, environments, and delivery controls. This skill provides a structured workflow that helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code guide infrastructure planning.
actual_capabilities: Outlines phases for Terraform setup, provisioning, module creation, state management, environments, CI/CD, and security.
Lists related skills to invoke for Terraform, cloud architecture, CI/CD, and secrets management tasks.
Provides copy-paste prompts for guiding an AI assistant through Terraform workflow phases.
Defines quality gates for provisioned resources, working modules, configured state, tested environments, CI/CD, and security.
Helps structure Terraform planning conversations before implementation work begins.
limitations: Does not include Terraform source files, reusable modules, or provider-specific examples.
Does not execute Terraform commands or validate a live infrastructure plan.
Depends on separate referenced skills for detailed Terraform, CI/CD, and cloud architecture implementation.
Does not replace security review of generated Terraform code or cloud permissions.

use_cases.title: Start a Terraform Project
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to plan backend configuration, providers, variables, outputs, and first resource definitions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Reusable Modules
use_cases.description: Guide module interface design, documentation, outputs, and testing before sharing modules across teams.

use_cases.title: Prepare Multi-Environment Delivery
use_cases.description: Plan environment isolation, state access, CI/CD validation, approvals, and security gates.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Terraform Setup Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Terraform infrastructure workflow to plan a new Terraform project with backend, providers, variables, outputs, and initial quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Design Cloud Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Terraform infrastructure workflow to outline resource provisioning steps for networking, compute, storage, and required review checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Reusable Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Terraform infrastructure workflow to define a reusable module interface, required variables, outputs, documentation, and testing approach.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Environment Delivery Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Terraform infrastructure workflow to plan remote state, environment isolation, CI/CD validation, approvals, secrets handling, and access audit steps.

output_examples.input: Plan Terraform setup for a new cloud project.
output_examples.output: A phased setup plan covering backend selection, provider configuration, variables, outputs, initialization steps, and quality gates.

output_examples.input: Prepare a reusable module workflow for network resources.
output_examples.output: A module planning checklist with interface design, variables, outputs, documentation, test expectations, and release review points.

output_examples.input: Create a Terraform delivery workflow for dev, staging, and production.
output_examples.output: A delivery sequence covering environment structure, state locking, variable files, CI/CD validation, approvals, and security checks.

best_practices: Review generated Terraform plans before applying any infrastructure changes.
Use remote state with locking and restricted access for shared environments.
Pair this workflow with provider-specific review and secrets management guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not apply generated infrastructure changes without human review.
Do not store secrets directly in Terraform variables, state, or source files.
Do not use one state file or workspace strategy without considering environment isolation.

faq.question: Does this skill create Terraform code?
faq.answer: It provides workflow guidance and prompts. It does not include ready-made Terraform source files.

faq.question: Can it run Terraform commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is instructional Markdown and does not execute commands.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it provider-specific?
faq.answer: No. It describes a general Terraform workflow for cloud infrastructure planning.

faq.question: How should teams use the quality gates?
faq.answer: Use them as review checkpoints before merging, planning, or applying infrastructure changes.

faq.question: Does it handle secrets safely?
faq.answer: It prompts users to include secrets management, encryption, policies, compliance, and access audit steps.
